ORDER SUSTAINING SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST, SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION
MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-27-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD, FOURTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS DO NOT OPPOSE THE DEMURRER AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD, FOURTH, AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND STATE THAT THEY HAVE ELECTED NOT TO PURSUE THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION. THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES ONLY CONCLUSIONS, NOT FACTS. NO FACTS ARE ALLEGED WHY THE TRANSFER TAX "WAS DONE ARBITRARILY AND ILLEGALLY." SPECIFICALLY, THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ALLEGE ANY FACTS WHY EITHER: A) THE TRANSFER TAX IS IRRATIONAL UNDER THE RATIONAL BASIS TEST CURRENTLY USED TO DETERMINE WHETHER GOVERNMENTAL ACTION NOT INVOLVING PROTECTED CLASSES OR FUNDAMENTAL RIGHTS PASSES MUSTER UNDER THE FEDERAL AND CALIFORNIA EQUAL PROTECTION CLAUSES AND/OR B) THE TRANSFER TAX SHOULD BE REVIEWED UNDER A TEST DIFFERENT THAN RATIONAL BASIS AND DOES NOT MEET THAT TEST. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR #11332, BUS. TEL: 415-551-3745. (D302)

COURT TRIAL SET FOR FEB-04-2019 CONTINUED TO JUN-03-2019 AT 11:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 220 FOR STATUS HEARING. OPENING BRIEFS SHALL BE NO MORE THAN 25 PAGES, REPLY BRIEFS SHALL BE NO MORE THAN 10 PAGES. THE COURT DIRECTS COUNSEL TO SUBMIT THE FOLLOWING BRIEFS ON THE FOLLOWING DATEES: OPENING BRIEFS DUE ON APRIL 5, 2019; RESPONSE/ OPPOSITION BRIEFS ARE DUE MAY 3, 2019; AND REPLY BRIEFS ARE DUE MAY 17, 2019.PROPOSE STATEMENT OF DECISION (FROM BOTH SIDES) DUE MAY 28, 2019. STATUS HEARING SHALL BE HEARD JUNE 3, 2019 (AT 11:00AM). HEARING SHALL TAKE PLACE ON JUNE 24, 2019 AT 9:00AM. PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. JUDGE: KATHLEEN KELLY; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY (D220).

REMOVED FROM COURT TRIAL CALENDAR ON JUN-24-2019 IN DEPT. 220 - THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LEGAL ENTITY TRANSFER WAS A CHANGE IN ONWERSHIP OF THE SUBJECT PROPERTY AND, THEREFORE, SUBJECT TO THE TRANSFER TAX. MOREOVER, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE TRANSFER TAX VIOLATES NEITHER THE U.S. NOR THE CALIFORNIA CONSTITUTIONS. AHLP AND ASHFORD SF'S CLAIM FOR A REFUND IS DENIED, AND JUDGMENT IN THIS MATTER SHALL BE ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE CITY. THE CITY IS ORDERED TO PREPARE JUDGMENT IN THIS MATTER. JUDGE: KATHLEEN KELLY; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY (D220).

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Thomas A. Nuris, Esq. (650) 756-0225) appears for plaintiff/opposing party, Ashford Hospitality LLP

Attorney, Richard J. Ayoob of Ajalat Polley Ayoob et al (818-553-1300) appears for plaintiff/opposing party, Ashford Hospitality LLP

Deputy City Attorneys, Carole F. Ruwart and Rebecca Bers (415-554-4666) appear as counsels for defendant/moving party, City and County of San Fransico

Court clarifies tentative tuling as set forth on the record. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST, THIRD, FOURTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the first, third, fourth, and sixth causes of action and with twenty days leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. Plaintiffs do not oppose the demurrer as to the first, third, fourth, and sixth causes of action and state that they have elected not to pursue those causes of action. The seventh cause of action alleges only conclusions, not facts. No facts are alleged why the transfer tax "was done arbitrarily and illegally." Specifically, the seventh cause of action does not allege any facts why either: a) the transfer tax is irrational under the rational basis test currently used to determine whether governmental action not involving protected classes or fundamental rights passes muster under the federal and California equal protection clauses and/or b) the transfer tax should be reviewed under a test different than rational basis and does not meet that test. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332, Bus. Tel: 415-551-3745. (D302)
